bullets.text: The US president has reinstated an Iran port blockade and vowed a 20% charge on all cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
A massive fire at a Bangkok bar has killed at least 28 people and critically injured 25 others, with firefighters arriving to find patrons fleeing through flames.
Developing countries are reportedly spending more on repaying foreign debt than on education, with a UN report indicating that children in 113 countries lost out to debt servicing.
A man has been charged with murder in Sydney after an elderly man was allegedly attacked during a morning walk.
Police are investigating a hit-and-run incident south of Brisbane that resulted in one fatality.
Authorities are warning hikers against relying on social media influencers for winter bushwalking advice, particularly in Tasmania.

Oil prices surged and global markets experienced drops in stocks and bonds following escalating tensions between the US and Iran, as the US reinstated a blockade on Iranian ships and proposed a 20% charge for all cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
A top Federal Reserve official has warned that persistently high inflation could lead to further interest rate hikes, impacting market stability.
Citigroup is undergoing a significant restructuring under CEO Jane Fraser, involving asset sales, job cuts, and new hires, as the bank aims to regain its prominence.
